framing: This interview guide is designed to gather insights and understand individual perspectives on the green transition. It involves questions about personal details, basic understanding, attitudes, and perceived impact of the green transition.
introduction:
Welcome and thank you for participating in this short interview about the green transition.

The questions will cover a range of areas including your own understanding of the green transition, your attitudes towards it, and how you think it may impact your future.

Your perspectives are invaluable to us, and we encourage you to share openly and honestly and give as elaborate answers as possible.

questions.main_question: How old were you when you first started thinking about the green transition?
What do you think about this horse?
sub_questions: How does the horse's disability make you feel?
How do you think the horse feels about its disability?
How do you think it impacts the horses life and relationships?

image.primer: I will now present you with an image.
image.name: horse.jpg
image.description: The image is of a horse with two legs running in a field.
image.alt: A horse running in a fied with two legs.

questions.main_question: Do you recall any specific event or piece of information that sparked your interest in the green transition?
sub_questions: Was this event local, national, or international?
How did it change your perspective on environmental issues?

questions.main_question: Could you share your understanding of what the green transition means?
sub_questions: What are the main components of the green transition in your view?
How do you see its relevance to your daily life?

questions.main_question: How would you describe your overall attitudes towards the green transition?
sub_questions: What motivates your attitudes?
Have your attitudes changed over time?

questions.main_question: What do you perceive as the main benefits of the green transition?
sub_questions: Can you provide specific examples of these benefits?
Are there certain benefits that you value more than others?

questions.main_question: Are there any drawbacks or concerns you have associated with the green transition?
sub_questions: How do you think these drawbacks could be mitigated?
Do you discuss these concerns with your peers?

questions.main_question: In what ways do you believe the green transition will affect your personal life and your professional life?
sub_questions: What are the most significant changes you anticipate?
How are you preparing for these changes?

questions.main_question: Do you feel that as an individual you have a role to play in the green transition?
sub_questions: How do you define your personal responsibility in this transition?
What barriers do you face in contributing to the green transition?

questions.main_question: Could you please share any actions or practices you have incorporated into your lifestyle that correlate with the green transition?
sub_questions: How have your habits changed due to the green transition?
Do you face challenges in maintaining these practices?

questions.main_question: In your opinion, what can policymakers do to successfully drive the green transition?
sub_questions: What kind of policies have you seen effective in other contexts?
How can stakeholders be engaged to support these policies?

questions.main_question: How do you believe the green transition will transform our society as a whole in the long run?
sub_questions: What are the long-term societal benefits you anticipate?
Do you have any concerns about societal resistance to the green transition?

questions.main_question: Do you see any major obstacles or challenges society could face due to the green transition?
sub_questions: What strategies do you think could overcome these obstacles?
How can society balance the needs of the present with the goals of the green transition?
